factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: These reductions were primarily due to the current economic environment principally driven by the COVID-19 pandemic which has resulted in lower flight hours, aircraft fleet utilization and commercial OEM deliveries.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin military sales.

evidence.text: The organic sales decrease of $2.2 billion in the quarter ended September 30, 2020 compared to the quarter ended September 30, 2019 primarily relates to lower commercial aerospace aftermarket sales of $1.3 billion, including declines across all aftermarket sales channels, and lower commercial aerospace OEM sales of $1.0 billion. These reductions were primarily due to the current economic environment principally driven by the COVID-19 pandemic which has resulted in lower flight hours, aircraft fleet utilization and commercial OEM deliveries. This decrease was partially offset by higher military sales of $0.1 billion.
evidence.subsection: Quarter Ended September 30, 2020 Compared with Quarter Ended September 30, 2019

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin military sales indicates a positive development.

These reductions were primarily due to the current economic environment principally driven by the COVID-19 pandemic which has resulted in lower flight hours, aircraft fleet utilization and commercial OEM deliveries. factors.summary: Near-term risks include the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and potential government budget uncertainties.

evidence.text: The organic sales decrease of $2.2 billion in the quarter ended September 30, 2020 compared to the quarter ended September 30, 2019 primarily relates to lower commercial aerospace aftermarket sales of $1.3 billion, including declines across all aftermarket sales channels, and lower commercial aerospace OEM sales of $1.0 billion. These reductions were primarily due to the current economic environment principally driven by the COVID-19 pandemic which has resulted in lower flight hours, aircraft fleet utilization and commercial OEM deliveries.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence highlights significant negative impacts from the COVID-19 pandemic and economic downturn.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange rates and government budgets are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Backlog is affected by changes in foreign exchange rates. Bookings are impacted by the timing and amounts of awards in a given period, which are subject to numerous factors, including: (1) the desired capability by the customer and urgency of customer needs, (2) customer budgets and other fiscal constraints, (3) political and economic and other environmental factors, (4) the timing of customer negotiations, (5) the timing of governmental approvals and notifications, and (6) the timing of option exercises or increases in scope.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange rates and government budgets pose moderate risks, indicating potential headwinds.

evidence.text: Defense bookings were approximately $8.4 billion and $3.6 billion for the quarters ended September 30, 2020 and 2019, respectively, and approximately $21.8 billion and $12.4 billion for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 and 2019, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows a clear increase in defense bookings, indicating a positive trend.

evidence.text: Defense backlog was approximately $70.2 billion and $22.3 billion as of September 30, 2020 and December 31, 2019, respectively.

sentiment.rationale: The significant growth in contract backlog suggests a very positive development.
